title: Which external-library prefixes should be textual versus color-only?
summary: The current policy hides many GLFW, Vulkan, CUDA, ImGui, and related prefixes but colors SDL_ without hiding it, and external corpus captures make the asymmetry visible.
details: Prefix conceal is a visual-language choice, not a correctness repair, as long as classify-before-strip preserves provenance.
SDL_Renderer currently remains SDL_Renderer in parameter overlay output while GLFWmonitor becomes monitor, VkSurfaceKHR becomes SurfaceKHR, and CUDA complex types become cf32/cf64.
The owner has explicitly selected hiding qnpeps:: and the existing Qnpeps/qnpeps_/QNPEPS_/qn_ identifier families; that subcase is no longer open.
A future style-lab round should compare representative SDL, GLFW, Vulkan C/Vulkan-Hpp, CUDA, and ImGui code before changing families in bulk.
Any selected hiding rule must apply consistently to raw matches, overlay expressions, type chunks, return movement, alignment width models, and snippet inverses where relevant.

provenance.reference: conversation 2026-07-11 request to mix SDL, GLFW, and Vulkan code into the lab
provenance.note: Owner requested cross-style examples while reviewing CUDA prefix conceal and color.
The current family asymmetry is directly visible in actual output.

evidence.claim: A reproducible comparison corpus now exists for a later decision.

questions: Should SDL_ be hidden like GLFW?
Which external prefix decision remains open?
What corpus should be used to choose library prefix rules?

last_verified.method: Current strip/match code and pinned real-grid external corpus compared; no owner choice inferred.
